title: Find the Middle Node of a Linked List
description: Given a linked list, return the middle node. If there are two middle nodes, return the second middle node. You can define a linked list node as follows: class ListNode { int val; ListNode next; }

examples.explanation: The linked list has an odd number of nodes (5). The middle node is the third node with value 3, and all nodes after it (3, 4, 5) are returned.

The linked list has an even number of nodes (6). The two middle nodes are 3 and 4. According to the problem statement, we return the second middle node, which is 4, along with all subsequent nodes (4, 5, 6).
The linked list has only one node (1). This node is the middle node by default, and thus is returned.

constraints: The number of nodes in the linked list is in the range [1, 100].
Each node's value is within the range [-100, 100].
hints: Consider using two pointers: one fast and one slow.
The slow pointer moves one step at a time, while the fast pointer moves two steps.
When the fast pointer reaches the end of the list, the slow pointer will be at the middle.
Think about how to handle lists with an even number of nodes.
